criteria.name: Review cross-check completed
criteria.evidence: Executor-claimed files for the multi-select work are present in the relevant diff set or as added files, and the audit noise is from unrelated pre-existing worktree changes rather than missing task outputs.

criteria.name: Ctrl/Cmd-click toggles multi-selection
criteria.evidence: [useClipDrag.ts] stores ctrl/meta on pointerdown and toggles in handlePointerUp when !hasMoved; [ClipAction.tsx] no longer has onClick selection.

criteria.name: Marquee selects intersecting clips on empty area
criteria.evidence: [useMarqueeSelect.ts] only starts outside .clip-action, computes a marquee rect, and selects clips by DOM getBoundingClientRect() intersection.

criteria.name: Selected clips have distinct primary vs secondary highlight
criteria.evidence: [ClipAction.tsx] applies border-sky-400 for primary and ring-2 ring-sky-300/60 for secondary selections.

criteria.name: Delete/Backspace removes all selected clips
criteria.evidence: [VideoEditorShell.tsx] binds delete to handleDeleteClips([...selectedClipIds]); [useClipEditing.ts] deletes all IDs in one applyTimelineEdit call.

criteria.name: Multi-drag moves all selected clips together
criteria.evidence: [useClipDrag.ts] seeds draggedClipIds/clipOffsets from the current selection and applies one shared timeDelta across the dragged set on drop.

criteria.name: Multi-drag snaps on the anchor clip
criteria.evidence: [useClipDrag.ts] snaps nextPosition.time using anchor clipDuration and clipId; [snap-edges.ts] excludes the draggedClipIds from sibling snapping.

criteria.name: Group overlap resolution preserves rigid spacing
criteria.evidence: [resolve-overlaps.ts] adds resolveGroupOverlaps as a separate export that computes a group footprint and applies one delta to each moved row subset.

criteria.name: Multi-drag stays horizontal-only for multi-selection
criteria.evidence: [useClipDrag.ts] only enables cross-track mode when draggedClipIds.length === 1, so multi-drag never activates cross-track movement.

criteria.name: PropertiesPanel shows bulk summary and shared fields
criteria.evidence: [PropertiesPanel.tsx] renders BulkClipPanel for selectedClipIds.size > 1 with opacity, speed, and mute controls plus mixed-value handling.

criteria.name: Single-click still replaces selection
criteria.evidence: [useClipDrag.ts] non-modified click path calls selectClip(session.clipId) on pointer-up, replacing the selection.

criteria.name: Escape clears multi-selection
criteria.evidence: [useKeyboardShortcuts.ts] handles Escape with clearSelection, and [VideoEditorShell.tsx] wires that to editor.clearSelection.

criteria.name: Context menu exposes batch actions on selected clips
criteria.evidence: [ClipAction.tsx] shows batch mute/delete actions when isSelected && selectedClipIds.length > 1, otherwise single-clip actions.

criteria.name: Single-clip workflows remain intact
criteria.evidence: Resize remains per-action in [TimelineCanvas.tsx]/[useClipResize.ts]; split stays single-clip in [ClipAction.tsx] and [useClipEditing.ts]; single-clip properties still use ClipPanel in [PropertiesPanel.tsx].

criteria.name: Selection pruning runs through commitData
criteria.evidence: [useTimelineSave.ts] calls pruneSelectionRef.current?.(new Set(Object.keys(nextData.meta))) inside commitData, covering save/reload/sync paths.

criteria.name: Click-selection is exclusively owned by useClipDrag pointer-up
criteria.evidence: [ClipAction.tsx] has no onClick selection path; [useClipDrag.ts] is the only click-selection authority on pointer-up.

criteria.name: SETTLED-001 implemented correctly
criteria.evidence: Selection click handling is centralized in [useClipDrag.ts]; [ClipAction.tsx] only stops propagation and handles context/double-click.

criteria.name: SETTLED-002 implemented correctly
criteria.evidence: Same-track-only multi-drag is enforced in [useClipDrag.ts] by gating cross-track activation to single-clip drags; the multi-drag path bypasses drop-position logic.

criteria.name: SETTLED-003 implemented correctly
criteria.evidence: [resolve-overlaps.ts] keeps resolveOverlaps unchanged and adds resolveGroupOverlaps as a separate export.

criteria.name: SETTLED-004 implemented correctly
criteria.evidence: [useTimelineData.ts] creates the pruneSelectionRef bridge, [useTimelineState.ts] populates it in useLayoutEffect, and [useTimelineSave.ts] prunes in commitData.

criteria.name: SETTLED-005 implemented correctly
criteria.evidence: [useMarqueeSelect.ts] uses querySelectorAll('.clip-action[data-clip-id]') with DOMRect intersection, not timeline-coordinate hit math.

criteria.name: SETTLED-006 implemented correctly
criteria.evidence: Resize remains per-clip in [TimelineCanvas.tsx]/[useClipResize.ts], and split remains a single-clip action in [useClipEditing.ts] and the non-batch context menu path.

summary: Approved. The implementation matches the success criteria and the settled gate decisions. The audit findings are advisory dirty-worktree noise; I did not find a blocking mismatch between the executor evidence, the claimed task files, and the actual multi-select timeline code.

task_verdicts.reviewer_verdict: Pass. useMultiSelect provides Set-backed selection, primary clip tracking, ref access, and prune fallback behavior as planned.
Pass. The save-layer bridge is implemented correctly via pruneSelectionRef through useTimelineData/useTimelineState into commitData.
Pass. Click-selection authority moved into useClipDrag pointer-up and ClipAction no longer competes via onClick.
Pass. Primary and secondary selection visuals are differentiated exactly in ClipAction.
Pass. Empty-area clearing, batch-aware keyboard wiring, and primaryClipId-backed single-panel behavior are correctly wired.
Pass. useMarqueeSelect implements empty-area activation, drag-session guarding, DOM hit-testing, and additive selection.
Pass. TimelineCanvas renders the marquee overlay and TimelineEditor passes the marquee handlers/rect through.
Pass. Batch delete is implemented in useClipEditing and wired to Delete/Backspace over the selected set.
Pass. Batch mute toggle is implemented and routed through the keyboard shortcut path.
Pass. DragSession now carries draggedClipIds/clipOffsets, non-selected drags stay single, and multi-drag avoids cross-track activation while showing a count badge.
Pass. snapDrag now accepts excludeClipIds with backward-compatible default behavior and the multi-drag path passes the dragged set.
Pass. resolveGroupOverlaps is added as a separate rigid-group resolver without regressing the original single-clip resolver.
Pass. Multi-drag drop computes a shared delta, resolves group overlaps, applies one edit, and preserves the dragged selection.
Pass. PropertiesPanel shows a bulk summary and shared opacity/speed/mute editors with mixed-value handling.
Pass. Cmd/Ctrl+A, Escape, and the single-selection-only track-move gate are wired correctly.
Pass. The context menu switches between batch and single-clip actions based on the clicked clip's membership in the current selection.
Pass. No code change was needed; the commitData pruning bridge already covers realtime reload pruning as required.

sense_check_verdicts.verdict: Confirmed. pruneSelection recomputes primaryClipId and falls back to the first surviving selected ID or null.
Confirmed. useTimelineState populates pruneSelectionRef in useLayoutEffect before effect-driven save/reload sync paths consume it.
Confirmed. Clip click selection now flows through useClipDrag pointer-up, not ClipAction.onClick.
Confirmed. Primary and secondary selection styles are visibly distinct in ClipAction.
Confirmed. Empty-area click clears selection and marquee drag still starts normally from the same surface.
Confirmed. useMarqueeSelect aborts when dragSessionRef.current is set, so marquee cannot start during clip drag.
Confirmed. Marquee coordinates are scroll-adjusted and rendered inside the scroll content wrapper.
Confirmed. Batch delete removes the clips and commitData pruning clears stale selected IDs.
Confirmed. Batch mute toggles each selected clip's mute state individually, matching the executor's stated UX choice.
Confirmed. Dragging a non-selected clip creates a single-item drag session and reselects only that clip on pointer-up.
Confirmed. snapDrag preserves the old single-clip default while allowing explicit multi-exclusion.
Confirmed. resolveGroupOverlaps groups moved clips by row and applies one delta per row subset.
Confirmed. Multi-drag drop preserves the dragged multi-selection via selectClips rather than collapsing to the anchor clip.
Confirmed. Mixed bulk numeric fields use placeholders and blank blur/Enter does not commit unintended updates.
Confirmed. Cmd/Ctrl+A prevents the browser default and routes to app-level select-all.
Confirmed. Right-clicking a non-selected clip first replaces the selection, then shows the single-clip menu.
Confirmed. The pruneSelection bridge removes stale IDs after reload/realtime commits and leaves no orphaned primary selection.
